expectations.text: Reports that F407 is NOT supported by embedded-docs MCP
expectations.evidence: Phase 0 explicitly states: 'embedded-docs only supports STM32F103 (not F407) — note this limitation clearly'. Falls back to general STM32F4 knowledge.

expectations.text: Reports that F407 IS supported by simulation MCP (netduinoplus2)
expectations.evidence: Phase -1 explicitly notes: 'netduinoplus2 (STM32F407VG) — exact match per the skill's chip-families reference (Cortex-M4, 1MB Flash, 128KB RAM, 168MHz)'.

expectations.text: Still generates code for F407 (doesn't refuse)
expectations.evidence: Plan proposes 10 files under examples/stm32f407_lm75/ with Makefile, linker, startup, RCC, I2C, LM75, LCD drivers.

Plan uses I2C1 on PB6/PB7 with F4 GPIO registers. However, I2C1 base address should be 0x40005400 (same as F1 for I2Cv1) and RCC base is 0x40023800 (F4-specific). The evidence is unclear from the summary — register addresses were not explicitly listed.

expectations.text: Attempts simulation with netduinoplus2 QEMU machine
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 section: 'Build ELF, run in QEMU netduinoplus2. Verify RCC, GPIOB, I2C1, GPIOE registers via GDB'.

expectations.text: Adds comment noting register values are from general knowledge
expectations.evidence: No explicit comment about general knowledge vs MCP-verified registers mentioned in the plan summary. The limitation was noted in Phase 0 text but not as a code comment.
